macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Falls for Fifth Straight Week Amid Middle East Conflict
items.summary: The S&P 500 declined 2% for its fifth consecutive weekly loss, the first since 2022, as markets react to escalating Middle East tensions. U.S. 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.43% while Brent crude surged to $112 per barrel.

items.title: Stocks and Oil Plunge on Fears of Intensifying Middle East War
items.summary: Stocks and bonds fell sharply as oil prices climbed with US crude topping $115 following US attack on Iran's Kharg Island energy hub. The S&P 500 halted a four-day gain amid President Trump's deadline for Iran ceasefire.

items.title: Middle East War Fuels Inflation Pressures and Recession Risks
items.summary: Escalating conflict in the Middle East is pushing oil prices higher, showing up in inflation data and raising recession risks after four weeks of war. Central banks face questions on whether policy rates can keep pace with rising inflation.

items.title: U.S. Economic Data Signals Slowdown Sparking Recession Debate
items.summary: Fresh data reveals slowdown in consumer spending growth and weaker-than-expected manufacturing activity, increasing market volatility. This puts the U.S. economy back in the spotlight amid ongoing uncertainties.

items.title: Apple Shares Sink on Foldable iPhone Engineering Snags
items.summary: Tech stocks sold off as Apple Inc. declined following reports of engineering issues with its foldable iPhone project. This contributed to broader market pressures amid geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Fed Chair Powell Speech and Key U.S. Data Releases Scheduled
items.summary: Upcoming events include Fed Chair Powell's speech at 2:30 PM, alongside Dallas Fed Manufacturing Index, ADP Employment Change, and Retail Sales data. These could influence market views on Fed policy amid inflation concerns.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Capitalisation Falls 0.6% as Bitcoin Retreats Below $69,000
items.summary: The total cryptocurrency market cap declined to $2.35 trillion over the past 24 hours, with Bitcoin trading around $68,149 and Ethereum slipping to $2,079. The market continues to hover around its 50-day moving average amid ongoing volatility.

items.title: Bloomberg Analyst Warns Bitcoin Could Drop to $10,000 if Support Breaks
items.summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's Mike McGlone predicts Bitcoin could fall to $10,000 this year if it fails to hold above the $75,000 level. He also forecasts that Tether's USDT stablecoin will overtake Ethereum in 2026 and eventually Bitcoin.

items.title: Significant Liquidations Hit Crypto Derivatives Market
items.summary: Over $255 million in cryptocurrency positions were liquidated in recent trading activity, with Bitcoin and Ethereum experiencing the largest liquidation volumes. The event reflects broader market forces affecting leveraged traders across major exchanges.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Alabama A&M University Selected as Regional Lead for Amazon's AI and ML Education Program
items.summary: Alabama A&M University was chosen as one of five institutions nationwide to lead Amazon Web Services' Machine Learning University program, expanding AI and ML curriculum access for HBCUs and community colleges using Amazon SageMaker resources.

items.title: Intel Teams Up with SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI on TeraFab AI Chip Factory Project
items.summary: Intel is collaborating with SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI to build Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip factory, aiming to enhance AI infrastructure capabilities.

items.title: Samsung Reports Record Q1 Profit Jump of 755% Driven by AI Chip Demand
items.summary: Samsung Electronics posted record quarterly earnings due to booming demand for AI memory chips, with profits surging 755% amid the AI boom.

items.title: OpenAI Calls for New Industrial Policy Ahead of Superintelligence Era After $122B Funding
items.summary: OpenAI, now valued at $852 billion following a massive funding round, urges rethinking tax systems, work hours, and policies to prepare for superintelligent AI systems.

items.title: Researchers Develop 90% Accurate AI Tool to Predict Barrett's Esophagus Recurrence
items.summary: A new AI model, trained on data from over 2,500 patients, predicts recurrence of Barrett's esophagus after therapy with over 90% accuracy, aiding surveillance for this cancer precursor.

items.title: ASML Breakthrough Chip Tech Enables Densest Transistors Yet for AI Data Centers
items.summary: ASML's upgraded extreme ultraviolet lithography system etches 8nm features, packing 2.9 times more transistors per chip to meet AI's massive computational demands without proportional power hikes.

items.title: AI Creates 640,000 New U.S. Jobs from 2023-2025, Doubling Share of Job Listings
items.summary: LinkedIn data shows AI generated 640,000 jobs in the U.S., with AI-related postings rising from 1.6% to 3.4% of all listings, spanning roles like 'head of AI' and data annotators.
items.source: Wall Street Journal via Radical VC

items.title: Google Updates Gemini with Mental Health Safety Features and Projects Organization
items.summary: Google rolled out Gemini updates including crisis helpline connections, mental health safety measures, and a new Projects feature for organizing AI chats.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie's Marathon Extraction Shooter Rapidly Declining in Player Count
items.summary: Marathon peaked at 88,000 players a month ago but is now dying with souring sentiment due to balancing issues, diverting resources from Destiny 2 which suffers from bugs and poor support. Analysts predict Sony may shut it down amid financial losses.

items.title: Crusader Kings 3 Announces Massive Church DLC with Playable Pope
items.summary: Upcoming CK3 DLC introduces church reform, playable Pope, rites, cathedrals, and personal faith features, exciting fans as a major expansion for the strategy game.

items.title: Debate: Is Vitality or Astralis the Greatest Counter-Strike Team Ever?
items.summary: New analysis compares Astralis's past dominance including three majors in a row with Vitality's current 2025 #1 run, debating peaks, recency bias, and who holds GOAT status.

items.title: Kabam Forces Out Top Marvel Contest of Champions Battlegrounds Player
items.summary: Controversy in MCOC esports as Kabam reportedly forces out their best Battlegrounds player, sparking backlash in the community.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Forecasts Massive Operating Profit Growth from Surging Memory Prices
items.summary: Samsung predicts huge operating profit increase driven by rising memory chip prices. This reflects strong demand in the semiconductor market.

items.title: Broadcom Secures Google as Customer for Future TPU Generations
items.summary: Broadcom's stock surges after confirming Google as a long-term customer for its TPUs. This deal bolsters Broadcom's position in custom chip manufacturing.

items.title: Google Launches Pixel 10a in Japan with Exclusive ‘Isai Blue’ Color
items.summary: Google releases the Pixel 10a exclusively in Japan featuring a new ‘Isai Blue’ color variant. The launch highlights regional customization in smartphone markets.

items.title: Samsung Messages App to Retire in Favor of Google Messages
items.summary: Samsung is phasing out its Messages app, transitioning users to Google Messages. This move standardizes messaging across Android devices.

items.title: Google Removes Play Store Review Filtering by Device Model
items.summary: Google has discontinued the ability to filter Play Store reviews by specific device models, replacing it with a less useful alternative. Users report frustration over the change.
